name: Platinum Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Platinum. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Platinum surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Platinum's moderate reflectivity and thermal processing

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning with adequate energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage with controlled thermal input

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal while minimizing Platinum substrate heating

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for thorough cleaning of Platinum surfaces

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal accumulation

fluence.description: Energy density threshold for effective contaminant removal without Platinum damage

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of precious metals like platinum to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for light contamination. Based on CleanLaser and IPG Photonics specs for 1064 nm systems (e.g., CL 100 series).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal on reflective metals; higher for light contamination (e.g., oils), lower for heavy (e.g., oxides). Recommended 1500 mm/s balances efficiency and safety. From industry standards in laser cleaning manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser Systems guidelines).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of platinum with minimal HAZ due to its high thermal conductivity; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise work but less common industrially. 30 ns recommended. Supported by laser physics studies (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. on metal ablation thresholds).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning to control heat accumulation on high-reflectivity platinum; higher rates speed up process but risk buildup—40 kHz optimal trade-off. From academic literature (e.g., Opt. Lasers Eng. reviews on pulse repetition in metal cleaning).

energy_density.description: Fluence below platinum's ablation threshold (~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm) for safe contaminant removal; 1.5 J/cm² recommended to vaporize organics without substrate damage. Based on ablation threshold studies (e.g., Appl. Surf. Sci. on noble metals, noting Pt's high reflectivity reduces absorption).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots for detailed surface prep on platinum jewelry or components; 200 μm balances precision and coverage for industrial use. From equipment specs (e.g., SPI Lasers cleaning heads for fine metal work).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for thorough removal; 2 passes recommended for uniform platinum cleaning without overexposure. Drawn from industry case studies (e.g., laser cleaning of precious metals in J. Laser Appl.).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular platinum surfaces; 60% optimal for even energy distribution without excessive heat. From process optimization studies (e.g., Opt. Eng. on scan strategies for laser cleaning).
